item.title: The Trump coin and lessons from the Ostrogoths
item.description: The use of his image on a gold offering from the US Mint is a sign of how little power he actually has over America’s money

item.title: When dependence on America becomes a vulnerability
item.description: Allies have learnt that partnership with Trump’s US can endanger them

item.title: What the Iran war teaches America’s adversaries
item.description: The third Gulf war augurs a more anarchic world — and more dependence on a less credible US

item.title: Xerox copies a cheeky habit from debt restructurings past
item.description: Controversial tactic to raise new debt is unlikely to be popular with existing creditors

item.title: Time to tackle the rank absurdities in UK energy policy
item.description: The debate over North Sea oil and gas resembles a bar brawl between opponents who can’t see straight

item.title: Unlike Donald, I don’t know when to press the button
item.description: I’m buying back in, but who knows if I can pick the right day?

item.title: Do you really want your travel spots unspoilt?
item.description: The view from that remote beach is unimprovable — or is it?

item.title: Private credit’s stale pricing problem
item.description: More frequent asset valuations can boost market resilience and investor confidence

item.title: Corporate bonds are the new stocks
item.description: Plus, the defence of private credit

item.title: Private markets are developing their own Magnificent Seven
item.description: Investors are splashing cash at record rates, but the market has been getting narrower

item.title: Molecules are taking their revenge on services
item.description: Capital-intensive industries matter in the age of the Halo trade

item.title: Vanishing woodlands: where are the trees of Turgenev and Tolstoy?
item.description: A new book explores 900 years of forests and their history and culture — from Finland to Siberia to war-torn Ukraine

item.title: Lessons for rich families from a private banker
item.description: The secret to sustaining generational wealth isn’t financial wizardry but long-term character

item.title: Private credit’s moment for a healthy reset
item.description: Recent ructions highlight the case for better scrutiny of the asset class

item.title: Corebridge and Equitable tie-up goes for endurance more than flair
item.description: Unusually for a merger, the share prices of the two companies barely moved after the deal was announced

item.title: The US needs a low-cost airline consolidator, but JetBlue isn’t it
item.description: Heavily indebted, unprofitable carrier might not make an ideal partner

item.title: OpenAI makes a ‘Code Red’ turn in strategy
item.description: The company shows some business discipline in ditching Sora video app and plans for erotic chatbot

item.title: Trump’s war is delivering a gut punch to the US economy
item.description: A supply chain crisis will hit ordinary Americans with higher prices at a time when they are already feeling stretched

item.title: The relentless march of the omniscalers
item.description: Tech giants such as Alphabet, Amazon, Alibaba and Huawei are commanding enormous global resources

item.title: Should Sweden finally adopt the euro?
item.description: The case against membership of the single currency looks much less clear-cut than it did in the 1990s

item.title: Will software engineers survive agentic AI?
item.description: A data deep dive shows that job vacancies are rising — but only for senior developers

item.title: The case for keeping Keir Starmer a little longer
item.description: Panic-buying a slightly less unpopular rival will not help Labour face its real challenges

item.title: The way out of WTO gridlock
item.description: Governments need to copy, bypass and borrow from the institution where they can

item.title: The EU economy’s slow-burning problem
item.description: Sluggish private R&D spending across Europe is hurting competitiveness, data suggests

item.title: How conflict is driving hunger
item.description: The Swede who runs the World Food Programme on its hardest year for decades
